title: [apiv2]  unable to copy to response : "write tcp 127.0.0.1:8080->127.0.0.1:50680: write: connection reset by peer"
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080

2. ./bin/podman pull alpine

3. curl -v -s http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/images/alpine/get

**Describe the results you received:**

```
1. terminal window 1
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080 &
[1] 91815
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ This function is EXPERIMENTAL.

2. terminal window 2
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ curl -v -s http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/images/alpine/get
*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> GET /v1.40/libpod/images/alpine/get HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
>
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/octet; charset=us-ascii
< Date: Thu, 14 May 2020 03:36:02 GMT
< Transfer-Encoding: chunked
<
* Failed writing body (0 != 16384)
* Failed reading the chunked-encoded stream
* Closing connection 0
**Describe the results you expected:**

3. return terminal window 1
ERRO[0021] unable to copy to response: "write tcp 127.0.0.1:8080->127.0.0.1:50704: write: connection reset by peer"
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
```
[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ps axuf|grep -i '[P|p]odman'
fedora     91815  0.0  0.8 1491556 32440 pts/2   Sl   03:35   0:01              \_ ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080
fedora     91824  0.0  1.3 1424164 54036 pts/2   Sl   03:35   0:02              |   \_ ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080
fedora     91827  0.0  0.0      0     0 ?        Zs   03:35   0:00              |       \_ [podman] <defunct>
fedora     91828  0.0  0.0  42336  1220 ?        S    03:35   0:00 ./bin/podman
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            2.0.0-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
Git Commit:         fa5b33e70b96ea8dfca3b46cab44a2e07028bf0c
Built:              Thu May 14 03:21:15 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Openstack VM

comments.body: remove the -s and you will find that the -s is covering up an error.  the error is that this endpoint returns a binary stream (the image), it encourages you to use -o or redirect your output.  For this sort of thing, i would recommend using -o as to not mask any errors.  I would also drop -s from the standard incantation of curl.

> 
> 
> remove the -s and you will find that the -s is covering up an error. the error is that this endpoint returns a binary stream (the image), it encourages you to use -o or redirect your output. For this sort of thing, i would recommend using -o as to not mask any errors. I would also drop -s from the standard incantation of curl.

I only saw the path parameter is required in http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/libpodExportContainer, so I originally think there is a default exported path, but it's seems not, it means users must use -o or redirect their output. in addition, IMO, I also need to improve this for the following error even though users forgot output path option w/ curl or it's a purpose behavior.
```
ERRO[0021] unable to copy to response: "write tcp 127.0.0.1:8080->127.0.0.1:50704: write: connection reset by peer"

[fedora@fedora32 libpod]$ ps axuf|grep -i '[P|p]odman'
fedora     91815  0.0  0.8 1491556 32440 pts/2   Sl   03:35   0:01              \_ ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080
fedora     91824  0.0  1.3 1424164 54036 pts/2   Sl   03:35   0:02              |   \_ ./bin/podman system service --timeout 0 tcp:127.0.0.1:8080
fedora     91827  0.0  0.0      0     0 ?        Zs   03:35   0:00              |       \_ [podman] <defunct>
fedora     91828  0.0  0.0  42336  1220 ?        S    03:35   0:00 ./bin/podman
```

NOTE: as you seen, the podman process is defunct state.

BTW, it works for me to run curl w/ -o option.
```
[fedora@fedora32 ~]$ curl -v -o /tmp/foobar http://localhost:8080/v1.40/libpod/images/alpine/get
  % Total    % Received % Xferd  Average Speed   Time    Time     Time  Current
                                 Dload  Upload   Total   Spent    Left  Speed
  0     0    0     0    0     0      0      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:--     0*   Trying ::1:8080...
* connect to ::1 port 8080 failed: Connection refused
*   Trying 127.0.0.1:8080...
* Connected to localhost (127.0.0.1) port 8080 (#0)
> GET /v1.40/libpod/images/alpine/get HTTP/1.1
> Host: localhost:8080
> User-Agent: curl/7.69.1
> Accept: */*
>
* Mark bundle as not supporting multiuse
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
< Content-Type: application/octet; charset=us-ascii
< Date: Sat, 16 May 2020 03:46:20 GMT
< Transfer-Encoding: chunked
<
{ [4096 bytes data]
100 2838k    0 2838k    0     0  22.1M      0 --:--:-- --:--:-- --:--:-- 22.3M
* Connection #0 to host localhost left intact
[fedora@fedora32 ~]$ ls -lah /tmp/foobar
-rw-rw-r--. 1 fedora fedora 2.8M May 16 03:46 /tmp/foobar
[fedora@fedora32 ~]$ file /tmp/foobar
/tmp/foobar: POSIX tar archive
```

Thanks!
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@chuanchang @baude What is the state of this issue? Can I close it?
